title: Hardware set up
author: Past-News9538
selftext:
Trying to summon people that are smarter than me for this one:

Trying to decide on my hardware set up for an ai agent for trading. I know i dont need anything too complicated to vibecode a moving average strategy as a civilian. but i am also trying to keep my thoughts into the future.  i am also trying not to think as a human, but as an agent. The brain will evolve exponentially, so i am not looking at what it can do today, but what it can do in 3 years and i am thinking it might be worth getting a body that will be able to keep up.  
the way i see it: whatever its limits are today, they won't be there soon at some point, and it will be capable of developing models and strategies that we can't. sure i can get away with a hostinger vps for $8/month, but it will outgrow it at some point. and then what?  
at the same time, better models will be able to do more with less  
I guess i am trying to find a balance between future proofing and overkill

Give me your thoughts

comments.author: Interesting_Kiwi_417
comments.body:
I have been developing strategies since 2020, believe me that a machine with 16GB of ram and about 40 GB harddisck still helps me keep track of all my strategies and run it.

The main thing is that you need to learn how to architect your strategy to develop it. I use django to create my architecture and hence I am able to scale it. 

If you are looking for more information about how we can have multiple strategies running in parallel, then you should use celery to run the tasks. ( jobs) 

Regarding the api, you are going to be connected to any ai using api so that is not going to increase your body or size of your machine, ai simple gets called via api and works as simple as creating a chatbot on your machine.

Incase you are looking for VPS, I am happy to support .   
[https://quantbots.com/vps/](https://quantbots.com/vps/)

comments.author: Droppingdubs
comments.body: Happy to hop on a call and answer some questions, DM me

comments.author: Mammoth_Rain7941
comments.body:
Io al momento ho questi on premise. 

Airewebnode: 2x Xeon E5-2609, 128GB RAM, NVMe 476.9GB + storage raw 106TB su logical volumes, Linux Ub24 Server, attività: web node/storage node, archiviazione pesante, servizi backend, dataset, backup, file server, EVM-RPC node.

AirProd-LLM: Intel Core i9-14900KF, 128GB RAM, 3x RTX 3090 24GB (72Gb vRam), NVMe 931.5GB, Linux Ub24 Server, attività: produzione AI/AIR3, inference GPU, agent runtime, Plesk/app backend, modelli locali medio/grandi, servizi Agent principali.

DevGui: i7-14700KF, 64GB RAM, NVIDIA RTX 4070 Ti 12GB, nVME 2TB + swap 256GB, Ub24LTS, attività: workstation/dev locale GPU, test modelli piccoli/medi, sviluppo, scripting, esperimenti, supporto agent/tools

DevGui2: i7-7700K 32GB RAM, NVMe 238.5GB, Linux Ub24LTS, attività: dev GUI/frontend, AIRifica/AIRi3, test interfacce, bridge, dapp e servizi locali

UnrealSRV6: i7-11700K, 32GB RAM, RTX 5060 Ti 16GB, nVME 512GB, Windows 11 Pro, attività: Unreal/MetaHuman/streaming, rendering realtime, avatar 3D, OBS, produzione visuale AIR3, nodo modelli TTS

WebGLSRV8 : Intel Core i7-10700K 3.80GHz, 32GB RAM, NVIDIA RTX 3080 Ti, nVME 256GB, Windows 11 Pro, attività: streaming/rendering/Windows workstation, OBS, avatar/stream, test realtime, nodo modelli TTS/STT/visual.

comments.author: Thisizuser
comments.body: Mac mini would be good setup. Vibe coding most likely be in react so now browser has to be on all the time, better way is have cloud base server read data and have react to build strategy and execution, and communication should be sent to telegram
